# InfiniEdit

> InfiniEdit is a free software tool: a browser-based save-file editor for the sandbox crafting game Infinite Craft. It is a developer utility, not a game — it opens, edits, analyzes, and visualizes `.ic` save files. Unofficial fan project, not affiliated with Infinite Craft or its creators.

InfiniEdit is a single-file, no-build, no-framework HTML/CSS/JS web application. All core functionality runs client-side in the browser: no accounts, no analytics, no server-side storage of save data, nothing to install. It also exposes its editing actions as WebMCP tools (`document.modelContext` / `navigator.modelContext`) so an in-browser AI agent can inspect and edit the loaded save directly — call `get_save_summary` first, then `search_elements` / `get_element` to look things up, and `add_element` / `add_recipe` / `delete_element` / `export_save` to make changes.

## Docs

- [Full reference](https://infiniedit.qzz.io/llms-full.txt): complete technical reference covering the data model, localStorage keys, external API calls, full feature list, keyboard shortcuts, and the full WebMCP tool schema (inputs/outputs).
- [Homepage](https://infiniedit.qzz.io/): the live application. Requesting it with an `Accept: text/markdown` header returns the same content as llms-full.txt via content negotiation, at the same URL.
- [Source code](https://github.com/QikSeekDev/InfiniEdit): full client implementation, single `index.html`.
- [Issues / contribution](https://github.com/QikSeekDev/InfiniEdit/issues): bug reports and pull requests.

## Optional

- [Mirror](https://infiniedit.vercel.app): identical app, alternate deployment.
- [InfiniBrowser API](https://infinibrowser.wiki/api/Recipe?id=example): third-party recipe database InfiniEdit optionally queries for autocomplete and missing-recipe lookups (element names only, no save data sent).
- [WebMCP spec](https://webmachinelearning.github.io/webmcp/): the in-browser tool-calling standard InfiniEdit's AI tools are built on.
